franchisor_address: 8000 Tower, Suite 700, 8331 Norman Center Drive, Bloomington, MN 55437

concept_description.value: Quick service food restaurant with indoor seating selling the full line of approved DQ soft-serve, treat, food and beverage menu items. Two prototypical freestanding buildings: Next Gen Core 34 (1,938 sq ft, seats 34) and Next Gen Core 46 (2,208 sq ft, seats 46). Street locations (freestanding/strip) and Captive-venue locations (malls, airports, campus, etc.).

initial_franchise_fee.refund_conditions: Withdrawal before construction; training failure; no site in 90 days; no construction in 180 days

sales_promotion_fee.determination: ADQ determines within range; 90 days advance notice of adjustment

termination_fee.formula: 2× continuing license fee for last 12 months of active operations; or 24× average monthly if <12 months; or remaining months × average if <24 months remain

territory.franchisor_competition_rights: ADQ and affiliates may operate and franchise competing businesses at any location except the specific authorized location. May sell via internet, catalog, telemarketing, social media, mobile apps, third-party platforms without compensation.
territory.multi_unit_trade_area: MultiTRA grants non-exclusive development right with designated trade areas, subject to ADQ carve-outs for large venues, institutional locations, and pre-existing commitments

franchise_term.renewal_years: Shorter of 10 years or remaining lease term
franchise_term.renewal_conditions: Sign then-current agreement (may differ materially); modernize; good standing; pay $22,500 renewal fee; sign release

noncompete.during_term: No involvement in quick-service restaurant serving hamburgers (without alcohol) or business generating >10% revenue from frozen treats (Competitive Business)
noncompete.post_term: 500 meters from authorized location for 1 year (Street); same building/venue (Captive-venue)

dispute_resolution.method: Arbitration in Minneapolis, MN (except certain claims)
dispute_resolution.litigation_forum: Federal District Court for District of Minnesota or Hennepin County District Court, Minneapolis
dispute_resolution.governing_law: Law of state where authorized location is situated
dispute_resolution.state_overrides: Multiple state addenda modify dispute resolution (CA, IL, MD, MN, ND, WA)

schedule_a.population: Newly constructed freestanding restaurants, opened Jan 2014–Dec 2023, all 12 months reported, franchisee-owned
schedule_a.excludes: Texas DQ, conversions, fuel centers, non-traditional, territory operator subfranchised

schedule_b.manageable_profit_definition: Gross Sales minus COGS, Labor, and Restaurant Controllables. BEFORE occupancy, insurance, royalties, sales promotion fees, legal, accounting, admin.
schedule_b.sample_limitation: 2024 has only 55 usable P&Ls (285 excluded from 321 qualifying restaurants)

item_21_financials.guarantee: IDQ unconditionally guarantees ADQ's obligations to franchisees

notable_pending: Project Lonestar v. ADQ (N.D. Texas): breach of contract/fraud re transfer process
D'Elia named in Domino's securities class action (ADQ not a party)
ADQ v. Estate of Ridl (MN): termination for abandonment
Oshlo-Wilkinson v. ADQ (Iowa): territory transfer dispute

financing.notes: ADQ does not offer financing. May arrange introductions to third-party lenders.

training.location: Phase 1-2: certified DQ location; Phase 3: Minneapolis HQ

supply_chain.entity: Unified Supply Chain, Inc. (USCI), wholly owned by IDQ

advertising_fund.management_fee: 7% of sales promotion payments (not taken above 3% of gross sales)
advertising_fund.audit_type: Independent national accounting firm annual review

key_personnel.coo: Arthur P. D'Elia (COO US/Canada since Mar 2025; previously Domino's EVP International)
key_personnel.gc: Shelly O'Callaghan (EVP, GC, Secretary since Nov 2011)

item_20_notes.confidentiality_clauses: Some former franchisees have signed provisions restricting their ability to speak about their experience with ADQ

unresolveds.description: Item 19 2024 P&L sample: Only 55 of 321 qualifying restaurants (17%) provided usable P&Ls. Reason for 285 exclusions unclear.
Maryland fee deferral required by MD Securities Commissioner — suggests regulatory concern about ADQ financial condition
Confidentiality clauses restrict some former franchisees from speaking about their experience. Extent unknown.
Renewal requires then-current agreement which may differ materially — no disclosure of historical changes
Territory operators not bound by ADQ site clearance guidelines — unpredictable encroachment risk
No contractual limitation on frequency or cost of EPOS system upgrades
Dividends to Berkshire ($110M) exceed net income ($97.9M) in 2024, increasing retained deficit

contradictions.description: Cover page states initial franchise fee of $45,600 (includes MTRA); Item 5 states $45,000 + $600 MTRA separately. Consistent but bundling could mislead.
Item 19 Schedule A 2024 average ($1,427,281, 321 restaurants) vs Schedule B 2024 average ($1,648,666, 55 restaurants). Schedule B subset biased upward by $221K.
contradictions.resolution_status: unresolved — selection bias in P&L reporting

contradictions.description: Item 7 total matches cover page total ($1,516,200-$2,543,050). Consistent.

california.damages: Liquidated damages (termination fee) may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671
california.forum_selection: Arbitration venue may be challenged under CA B&P Code §20040.5
california.general_release: Release cannot waive CA Franchise Investment Law or Franchise Relations Act rights
california.anti_waiver: No waiver of state franchise law claims or disclaimer of reliance

hawaii.anti_waiver: No waiver of state franchise law claims or disclaimer of reliance

illinois.governing_law: Illinois law governs franchise agreement for IL franchisees
illinois.forum_selection: Forum selection outside IL void (except arbitration)
illinois.general_release: Release cannot negate IL Franchise Disclosure Act claims

maryland.fee_deferral: All initial fees deferred until outlet opens (MD Securities Commissioner requirement)
maryland.forum_selection: MD franchise law claims may be brought in Maryland
maryland.general_release: Release excludes MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law liability

michigan.termination: Good cause required with 30 days to cure; fair compensation on non-renewal
michigan.transfer: Cannot refuse transfer without good cause

minnesota.notice_cure: 90 days termination notice (60 days cure); 180 days non-renewal notice
minnesota.forum_selection: Cannot require litigation outside MN, jury waiver, injunctive relief consent, or liquidated damages
minnesota.general_release: Cannot require release waiving MN franchise law rights
minnesota.trademark_defense: ADQ must defend trademark infringement claims at ADQ's cost

north_dakota.damages: Termination fee DELETED entirely; punitive damages waiver unenforceable; jury waiver unenforceable
north_dakota.noncompete: May be unenforceable under ND law
north_dakota.forum_selection: Arbitration at nearest AAA office to authorized location, not Minneapolis
north_dakota.general_release: Release at renewal is not waiver of ND Franchise Investment Law liability

rhode_island.forum_selection: Venue/jurisdiction outside RI void for RI Franchise Investment Act claims

washington.governing_law: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in conflicts of law
washington.forum_selection: Arbitration in WA or mutual agreement; litigation in WA not precluded
washington.general_release: Release cannot include WA Act rights except negotiated settlement with independent counsel
washington.transfer: Transfer fees limited to reasonable estimated/actual costs
washington.noncompete: Void for employees earning <$100K/yr and independent contractors <$250K/yr
washington.no_poach: Cannot restrict franchisee from soliciting/hiring employees of other franchisees or franchisor
